name: Piedras Reservoir and Cartaya Loop
name_original: Desde Lepe hasta Embalse de Piedras – circular por Cartaya
description: Cycle 33.5 miles from Lepe to Embalse de Piedras via Cartaya, enjoying river views, pine forests, and historic sites like Castillo de los Zú
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Lepe offer a diverse landscape, blending coastal paths with inland pine forests and river views. The region, part of Spain's Costa de la Luz, features generally flat coastal areas and rolling hills further inland, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Marshlands in the vicinity add opportunities for nature observation. This combination of natural features makes Lepe suitable for a range of touring cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Lepe The most popular touring cycling route is Piedras Reservoir and Cartaya Loop, a 33.5 miles (53.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 41 minutes to complete. This route offers river views, pine forests, and passes by historic sites. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Antilla Beach – Hoyo Tower and Beach loop from Lepe, a moderate 22.7 miles (36.6 km) path. This route provides extensive coastal views along the Atlantic. Local touring cyclists also love the Beautiful forest section – Ecovia do Litoral loop from Lepe, a 89.3 miles (143.8 km) trail leading through scenic forest sections, often completed in about 9 hours 37 minutes. Touring cycling around Lepe is defined by coastal paths, pine forests, and marshlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to more challenging longer routes. The routes in Lepe are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 29 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Lepe's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Lepe?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes around Lepe, offering diverse landscapes from coastal paths to inland forests. You'll find options for various skill levels, including 13 easy, 11 moderate, and 6 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Lepe?
A: Lepe's touring cycling routes feature a mix of terrain. You can expect coastal paths with continuous sea views, tranquil sections through pine forests, and routes along riverbanks. The Costa de la Luz region is known for its generally flat coastal areas, with rolling hills inland that are not overly steep, making it suitable for road bikes and mostly paved surfaces.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Lepe offers several easy touring cycling routes. A good option for beginners or families is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33889089" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Antilla Beach – Hoyo Tower and Beach loop from Islantilla&lt;/a&gt;, which provides pleasant coastal views on a generally flat path.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see along the cycling routes?
A: Lepe's routes offer a variety of natural beauty and historical sites. You can cycle through marshlands rich in birdlife, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3970452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Odiel Marshes&lt;/a&gt;, or past serene bodies of water like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/982208" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Portil Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also lead through pine forests and along river views, with historical sites like Castillo de los Zú mentioned in the region's research.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Lepe area?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Lepe are designed as loops. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984451482/piedras-reservoir-and-cartaya-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedras Reservoir and Cartaya Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through pine forests and along river views, and the coastal &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3952597" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Antilla Beach – Hoyo Tower and Beach loop from Lepe&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Lepe?
A: The Costa de la Luz region, where Lepe is located, generally enjoys a mild climate. Spring and autumn are often ideal for touring cycling, offering comfortable temperatures and less crowded routes. While specific weather conditions can vary, the region's climate makes it suitable for cycling for a significant portion of the year, avoiding the peak heat of summer.

Q: Can I expect to see wildlife while cycling in Lepe?
A: Yes, the marshlands in the vicinity of Lepe are particularly rich in birdlife, offering excellent opportunities for nature observation during your cycling tours. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38278841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The marshes are rich in birdlife. – Water tank at the old railway track loop from Islantilla&lt;/a&gt; are specifically noted for this.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Lepe?
A: The touring cycling routes around Lepe are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 29 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from continuous sea views to tranquil forest sections, and the variety of routes suitable for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any challenging or advanced touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Lepe offers more difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38279110" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beautiful forest section – Ecovia do Litoral loop from Lepe&lt;/a&gt; is a longer, more demanding route that includes beautiful forest sections and is ideal for those looking for an extended ride.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints along the Lepe cycle routes?
A: Many coastal routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3952597" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Antilla Beach – Hoyo Tower and Beach loop from Lepe&lt;/a&gt;, offer extensive sea views across the Atlantic. Inland routes provide scenic vistas of pine forests and river landscapes. Lepe Country Park also features a café with sea views, serving as a good starting or stopping point for coastal rides.

Q: Where can I find information about parking for bike trails in Lepe?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not always listed, Lepe Country Park is a known starting point with facilities, including a café. For other routes, parking is generally available in the towns and villages along the routes, such as Lepe, Cartaya, or Islantilla, which serve as common start points.
tips: Views from the bridge of the Piedras River - a river of only 40 km that is located entirely in the province of Huelva, but at the same time has a fairly extensive basin.

This "White House" is the town hall of Cartaya, located in the Town Hall Square. 
It is a municipality in the province of Huelva with a population of 21,408 registered inhabitants, and is located in the estuary of the Cuadros River.

Built in the 15th century by Don Pedro Zúñiga, it was conceived to defend and monitor the land in the area that was regularly lashed by pirates. In the 16th century it was reinforced by adding defense elements and towards the end of the 17th century it was a refuge for citizens from Portuguese attacks. Its history as a fortress came to an end in 1812 and at some point it was planned to be demolished. Luckily today it is in perfect condition and has been declared an Asset of Cultural Interest in 1985.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route, spanning approximately 53.9 kilometers, typically takes around 3 hours and 41 minutes to complete for a touring cyclist with a good fitness level.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as moderate. It requires good fitness but is generally suitable for all skill levels due to its mostly paved surfaces. While there's a moderate elevation gain of about 348 meters, it's not overly strenuous for a well-prepared touring cyclist.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll find about 31.4 km of asphalt, 5.31 km of other paved surfaces, and 2.78 km of compacted gravel. There are also around 13.1 km of unpaved sections and 400 meters of cobblestones, which might require you to dismount and push your bike in some parts.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of natural beauty and cultural interest. You'll pass through the towns of Lepe and Cartaya. A significant natural highlight is the Piedras River, where you can enjoy views from a bridge and observe marshes known for their abundant birdlife. The route also traverses an extensive pine forest and passes irrigated fields supplied by the Embalse de Piedras. In Cartaya, you can see the Cartaya Town Hall and the historical Castillo de los Zúñiga.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Costa de la Luz region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and less chance of extreme heat or heavy rain, making for a comfortable ride.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As a circular route starting and ending in Lepe, you can typically find parking within the town of Lepe to begin your ride. Specific parking areas may vary, but public parking is usually available near the town center or residential areas.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas and towns, it's primarily designed for touring bicycles. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it is well-behaved, can keep pace with a bicycle, and is kept on a leash, especially in populated areas and near wildlife habitats. Always carry water for your pet and clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route. While parts of the route pass through the Paraje Natural Marismas del Río Piedras y Flecha del Rompido, access for cycling on designated paths is generally free and open to the public.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. You will cycle along parts of the Vía Verde del Litoral and the Camino de Servicio Canal del Piedras, among others, which contribute to the varied experience of the journey.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle ride on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and varied terrain, it's advisable to bring plenty of water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit (including a spare tube and pump), sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses), and appropriate cycling attire. A fully charged phone and a map or GPS device are also recommended.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drinks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through the towns of Lepe and Cartaya, you'll find opportunities to stop for refreshments. Both towns offer cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel and take a break.
